Quote/Text Content: I am focusing on an institutional analysis of nonprofit intermediary college access programs using neo-institutional and organizational theories - which is the organizational data I am using for the SSNA exercises.
I'd like to use SSNA to study the access organization's partnerships and reach within the U.S.
I hope to become more comfortable with quantitative methods for research and to become more proficient using R and other statistical software packages.
I also aim to develop a better understanding of the possible methods for modeling social networks
Potential uses of these models for demonstrating impacts and results to third-party stakeholders such as campus administrators, legislators, and the general public
I believe that this course will aid the research that I am conducting for my assistantship.
I am taking this course because I am interested in learning how to analyze networks using R.
I would like to learn how to expedite network analysis when using more than one network via R code.

I hope to be able to learn the code and stat packages necessary in order to be able to perform basic descriptive network analyses in R (centrality, density, etc), cluster analyses in R, analyzing/visualizing both two mode and one mode networks in R, key player analysis in R, and ERGMs in R.
